input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
    Title="MyMauiApp">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, its own flyout section -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — two tabs shown when this flyout item is selected -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — separate flyout entry -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create an AppShell.xaml for a .NET MAUI app with a specific navigation structure. Let me provide the code directly since this is a straightforward request for a code example.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout with two sections:
   - "Dashboard" (single page)
   - "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
2. Settings page accessible from the flyout
3. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

Let me create the AppShell.xaml and AppShell.xaml.cs files.

<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"

    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
    Title="MyMauiApp">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, its own flyout section -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — two tabs shown when this flyout item is selected -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — separate flyout entry -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the breakdown:

**`AppShell.xaml`** — visual hierarchy:

| Flyout entry | What it shows |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | Single `ShellContent` page |
| **Products** | Two `Tab`s — *Active* and *Archived* — rendered as bottom tabs |
| **Settings** | Single `ShellContent` page |

**`AppShell.xaml.cs`** — registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a non-visual route so you can push to it from any list:

```csharp
// pass a product id via query param
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

**Key concepts:**
- `FlyoutItem` = entry in the side menu.
- `Tab` inside a `FlyoutItem` = bottom tab within that section.
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` = detail pages not shown in the flyout but navigable via URI.
- `ContentTemplate` + `DataTemplate` = lazy-loads pages on first visit.

Make sure your page classes (`DashboardPage`, `ActiveProductsPage`, etc.) live in the `MyMauiApp.Pages` namespace to match the `xmlns:pages` declaration, and add matching icon files to `Resources/Images`.
